name: Neighborworks Green Bay
description_short: NeighborWorks Green Bay is a nonprofit housing counselor and community development organization offering homebuyer education, down payment assistance, and foreclosure prevention since 1982.
description_long:
NeighborWorks Green Bay is a resident-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation and Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) founded in 1982. As a member of the National NeighborWorks Network, the organization has become a pillar of housing assistance in northeastern Wisconsin, serving Green Bay and surrounding areas including Door County. Over four decades, the organization has educated and counseled 21,021 aspiring and existing homeowners while helping 3,869 families achieve homeownership through comprehensive support services.

The organization provides a broad range of homeownership services including HUD-aligned homebuyer education and counseling, down payment and closing cost assistance loans, purchase assistance programs, foreclosure prevention counseling, reverse mortgage education, and home improvement loans. Beyond counseling, NeighborWorks Green Bay engages in community-interest real estate development, managing properties for sale, rental properties, and commercial/retail spaces. They also operate several housing partnerships and lending corporations, including the Green Bay Housing Partnership, Door County Housing Partnership, Multifamily Mutual, and Workforce Housing Lending Corp. In 2025 alone, their homeownership center deployed $512,000 in loans to modest-income families.

What distinguishes NeighborWorks Green Bay is their integrated approach combining education, financial assistance, and real estate development. The organization has leveraged modest resources into $595 million in community investment since founding, renovated or built 466 homes and apartments, and developed over 50,000 square feet of nonprofit space. They offer bilingual services (English and Spanish homebuyer education classes) and maintain active community engagement through Good Neighbor Week, neighborhood associations, and volunteer opportunities. Their recent Give BIG Green Bay campaign raised $264,125—the highest total in that program's history—demonstrating strong community support.

NeighborWorks Green Bay is genuinely mission-driven with transparent governance and strong community ties. However, their services are geographically limited to Green Bay and surrounding areas, and specific eligibility requirements, interest rates for assistance loans, and counseling availability details are not fully detailed on their website. Prospective clients should contact them directly to understand current program capacity, wait times, and specific qualification thresholds for assistance.
pros: HUD-aligned homebuyer education and counseling services with 21,021+ residents educated since 1982
Direct lending program deployed $512,000 in down payment and closing cost assistance loans in 2025 alone
Bilingual homebuyer education classes offered in English and Spanish
Comprehensive support including foreclosure prevention, reverse mortgage education, and home improvement loans
Established 501(c)(3) CHDO with verified track record of helping 3,869 families achieve homeownership
Community-integrated approach with neighborhood associations, volunteer programs, and real estate development
Member of National NeighborWorks Network with transparent annual reports and leadership structure
cons: Services geographically limited to Green Bay area and Door County—not available statewide or nationally
Website lacks specific details on loan terms, interest rates, repayment periods, and eligibility requirements
No information provided about counseling wait times, appointment availability, or counselor certification details
Limited transparency on actual approval rates for assistance lending or what income thresholds qualify
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Green Bay/Door County seeking education and down payment assistance
Homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking to avoid default through prevention counseling
Spanish-speaking families seeking bilingual homebuyer education and homeownership resources
diagnosis: NeighborWorks Green Bay is ideal for Green Bay-area first-time homebuyers and struggling homeowners seeking free or low-cost HUD-aligned counseling and direct lending assistance. The main caveat is geographic limitation to northeastern Wisconsin and lack of detailed program eligibility/terms on their website—contact directly for current capacity and application requirements.
services: Down payment and closing cost assistance loans
Real estate development and property management
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 437 S Jackson St, Green Bay, WI 54301
